campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help pay for youth recreation scholarships. They also discussed extending a temporary local rule, and moved forward with plans to build a bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including applying for grant funding to help pay for it.
keyTopics: $4,000 donation to youth recreation scholarships from Water Tower Kitchen
Extension of temporary ordinance number 2326
New bicycle and pedestrian bridge planned over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
Grant application for bridge project funding

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public meeting on March 18, 2026, where residents had a chance to speak about a new rule (ordinance) the city is considering. After hearing from the public, the council moved forward with the proposal and decided to vote on officially adopting it at their next meeting instead of waiting for additional readings.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for community input on a proposed ordinance
Council waived standard first reading procedures to expedite the process
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption vote at next regular council meeting
Fast-tracked approval process used for this particular measure

los-altos.title: Los Altos City Council — March 24, 2026
los-altos.summary: Los Altos City Council met to finalize their 2026 weed control program and establish new rules for how the council operates. The council also approved flying Progress Pride and Juneteenth flags, discussed affordable housing policies, and heard from the regional transit authority about transportation planning.
keyTopics: Completion of 2026 weed abatement program
New City Council operating procedures and norms
Affordable housing unit transfer rules
Progress Pride and Juneteenth flag raisings
Housing Element Implementation and transit authority updates

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council approved several contracts and agreements to move forward with various projects and services. The main items included hiring a construction company (QLM, Inc.), updating existing agreements with several service providers, joining a statewide insurance group to potentially save money, and arranging an art transfer agreement.
keyTopics: New construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Joining California Joint Powers Insurance Authority for better insurance rates
Updates to agreements with Dudek, NHA, and other service providers
Art transfer agreement authorization

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 9, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council reviewed plans to support children and youth in the community, including updates on youth programs, summer safety initiatives, and intervention services. They also heard reports on code enforcement activities and discussed improvements to library facilities and services.
keyTopics: Youth Empowerment Alliance and programs helping young people
Safe Summer Initiative Grant to keep kids safe during summer months
Code Enforcement Annual Report on city violations and compliance
Library facility upgrades and customer experience improvements

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 7, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026 that allowed residents to participate both in-person and online. Residents could join the virtual meeting through Zoom or phone to watch and speak during public comment periods.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access via Zoom and phone
Public comment procedures for remote participants
How to raise hand and unmute for speaking

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 9, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Human Relations Commission held a meeting on April 9, 2026, where they honored the flag and heard personal stories from three commissioners about their experiences in Sunnyvale. The group also reviewed and approved the minutes from their previous meeting in March.
keyTopics: Human Relations Commission meeting and flag ceremony
Personal stories shared by Commissioners 5, 6, and 7
Approval of March 12, 2026 meeting minutes

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 8, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to discuss several community initiatives including a food and budget program presentation, a youth-led organization called Unmuted Tabs, and information about summer job opportunities for young people in the city. The council also approved minutes from their previous meeting and opened the floor for residents to speak about local issues.
keyTopics: Budget and Bites program presentation
Unmuted Tabs youth organization
City of Cupertino summer jobs opportunities
Public comment period for residents

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 7, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, using both in-person and online formats so residents could participate however works best for them. The city is making it easy for people to attend and give feedback either in person or remotely through Zoom or phone.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format - attend in person or join online via Zoom
Remote participation options via phone (1-669-900-6833)
Written public comment submission through eComment system on city website
City Council meetings continue to balance in-person and virtual access

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to approve several administrative and financial matters, including joining a state tax board program, considering fee waivers for a local educational organization, and awarding a construction contract for a city project. The council also reviewed funding plans and heard new item requests from individual council members.
keyTopics: Joining state Franchise Tax Board program with county partners
Fee waiver and donation request from Milpitas Community Educational organization
Construction contract award and project plan approval
Amendment to city's 5-year housing and community development plan
New workplan items proposed by council members

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to discuss and approve changes to the city's gas utility rates for the next fiscal year. The council reviewed the financial forecast for the gas utility and approved updates to the rate schedules that will affect residential and commercial gas customers starting in 2027.
keyTopics: Gas utility rate changes for residential customers (Schedule G-1)
Rate adjustments for commercial and master-metered properties (Schedule G-2)
Large commercial gas service rate updates (Schedule G-3)
Gas utility financial forecast and reserve fund transfers for fiscal year 2027
